The code is straightforward, static, and standard.\n- **Malicious activity:** No signs of malware or malicious intent.\n- **Obfuscation:** The code is clear, auto-generated, with no obfuscation.\n- **Vulnerabilities:** The code only provides static ABI and connection methods; no dynamic input processing or external data handling that could introduce vulnerabilities.\n\n### Scoring Justification:\n- **Malware score:** 0 — no malicious activity detected.\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 — code is straightforward and auto-generated.\n- **Risk score:** 0.1 — minimal risk, mainly because it facilitates blockchain interaction, which is inherently low-risk but not entirely risk-free if misused.\n\n### Recommendations:\n- The assigned scores are reasonable and consistent across reports.\n- The low security risk score (0.1) accounts for the fact that, while the code itself is safe, interacting with blockchain contracts always carries some inherent risk (e.g., connecting to malicious contracts), but this is outside the scope of this code snippet.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n**Malware score:** 0  \n**Obfuscated score:** 0  \n**Risk score:** 0.1\n\n**Note:** Since the code is purely a static, autogenerated wrapper with no malicious logic, I agree with the current scores.
